2606.06211.title: FiLM-Based Speaker Conditioning of a SpeechLLM for Pathological Speech Recognition
2606.06211.paper_summary: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) has advanced remarkably for standard speech; however, pathological speech from neurological conditions remains a significant challenge. We investigate speaker conditioning via Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM), injecting x-vector-derived information into each transformer layer of a frozen ASR encoder to adapt internal representations to individual pathological speakers without modifying base model weights. We benchmark this for the ASR task against standard and parameter-efficient fine-tuning baselines, complemented by post-processing, on Spanish and English pathological speech. Additionally, we evaluate if the adapted model preserves the ability to answer speech-related questions. Results show that speaker-conditioned ASR is competitive with established adaptation strategies while retaining performance on non-conditioned speech.
2606.06211.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音辨識（ASR）在標準語音方面已經取得了顯著的進展；然而，來自神經系統疾病的病理語音仍然是一個重大挑戰。我們通過特徵線性調制（FiLM）來研究說話者的調適，將基於 x-vector 的信息注入到凍結的 ASR 編碼器的每個Transformer層中，以便在不修改基礎模型權重的情況下，將內部表示調整為個別病理說話者。我們在西班牙語和英語的病理語音上，將此方法與標準和參數高效的微調基準進行比較，並輔以後處理。此外，我們評估調整後的模型是否保留回答與語音相關問題的能力。結果顯示，經過說話者調適的 ASR 在保持對非調適語音的性能的同時，與已建立的調適策略具有競爭力。
2606.06211.author: Fernando López et.al.
2606.06211.authors: Fernando López, Santosh Kesiraju, Jordi Luque
